title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds · Program Brief: Installed Smoke
summary: Bookbot works only from the repository root, imports an uninstalled local file, lacks metadata and version identity, builds from a dirty tree, and publishes a wheel never tested after installation. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the installed smoke increment in installable reproducible Bookbot package. Lear…
objectives: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.
Build wheel and source distribution from an identified clean revision and inspect contents, metadata, and excluded fixtures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-installed-smoke — Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-packaging-release-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-packaging-release-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_packaging_release_4(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"

# Evidence: bookbot-installed-smoke-w04oni
# Competency: bookbot-installed-smoke.

def evidence_bookbot_installed_smoke_w04oni(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "corpusw04oni",
        "policyino40w",
        "revision2702",
        "commandw04oni2702",
        "reportino40w2702",
        "test2702w04oni",
    )

    if not contents_inspected:

    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Installed Smoke model · Theory Installed Smoke, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Installed Smoke precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Installed Smoke model · Theory Installed Smoke, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 1668 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
